quick_facts.label: Current status
quick_facts.value: TISSNET is a legacy TISS entrance-exam label, not the active postgraduate admission test for the 2026–27 cycle. TISS’s current admissions materials use CUET-PG for eligible programmes, CAT 2025 for three named Mumbai programmes, and a separate Non-CUET route for specified blended-learning programmes.

quick_facts.label: Institution
quick_facts.value: Tata Institute of Social Sciences (TISS), a deemed-to-be university under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, administers its own programme admissions after the applicable entrance route.

quick_facts.label: 2026 CUET-PG selection basis
quick_facts.value: For the PG programmes covered by the CUET route, TISS states that CUET-PG 2026 score carries 100% weightage; it states there is no Extempore Reflection Test, group discussion, or online personal interview for those programmes.

quick_facts.label: Management-related exceptions in 2026
quick_facts.value: For 2026–27, TISS uses CAT 2025 scores—not TISSNET—for MA Human Resource Management & Labour Relations, MA Organisation Development, Change and Leadership, and Master of Hospital Administration at the Mumbai campus.

quick_facts.label: 2026 application timing
quick_facts.value: The published 2026 CUET-PG TISS-registration window was May 22 to June 11, 2026 (5:00 pm); this is past and dates, portals, and programme availability can change in later cycles.

sections.heading: What TISSNET means now
paragraphs: TISSNET historically referred to TISS’s own postgraduate entrance examination. For a current applicant, it should not be treated as an exam to register for in the 2026–27 cycle. TISS’s official 2026 PG materials state that admissions to its master’s programmes are based on CUET-PG 2026 scores except for three programmes using CAT 2025, while two specified blended-learning education programmes are presented under a Non-CUET route.

The node description is therefore only partly current: TISSNET was not limited to management admissions, and it is no longer the operative route for the current TISS PG cycle. Applicants should identify the exact TISS programme first and then follow its stated CUET-PG, CAT-PG, or Non-CUET procedure.
bullets: Use the official TISS admissions site rather than legacy TISSNET preparation pages or old notification PDFs.
For 2026–27, CAT applies to MA HRM & LR, MA ODCL, and MHA at Mumbai; eligible other listed master’s programmes use CUET-PG, subject to the programme’s published conditions.

sections.heading: Eligibility: check the programme and entrance route
paragraphs: There is no single current “TISSNET eligibility” rule to apply across TISS. TISS publishes programme-specific eligibility criteria and corresponding CUET-PG test-paper codes in its PG admissions materials. A candidate must meet the requirements of the particular programme as well as take the applicable entrance examination.

For the 2026 cycle, candidates for the three CAT-PG programmes needed CAT 2025, while applicants to eligible CUET-PG programmes were assessed through their CUET-PG 2026 score. TISS advises applicants to use its programme list and admission brochure for the precise requirements.
bullets: Confirm the degree/subject prerequisites for the target programme before applying.
Confirm the prescribed CUET-PG paper code where the programme is on the CUET route.
Do not assume that eligibility, testing route, or selection stages from a pre-2024 TISSNET notice remain valid.

sections.heading: Current assessment format
paragraphs: TISSNET has no current 2026 examination format because it is not the active TISS PG entrance route. For CUET-route TISS programmes, the relevant exam format is CUET-PG as conducted by the National Testing Agency; the TISS admission decision is based on CUET-PG 2026 score at 100% weightage for the programmes covered by TISS’s CUET selection criteria.

For the three CAT-PG programmes, applicants use CAT 2025 for the entrance-score component and should follow the TISS programme-specific selection process and calendar rather than any historical TISSNET pattern.
bullets: CUET-PG route: prepare according to the NTA CUET-PG paper applicable to the intended TISS programme.
CAT-PG route: prepare for CAT and monitor TISS’s separate selection and assessment notices.
Do not rely on old TISSNET sections, question patterns, or cut-offs as a current admission format.

sections.heading: How the 2026 process worked
paragraphs: For the CUET-PG route, the published 2026 calendar shows two distinct steps: registration for CUET-PG through the NTA process, followed by TISS’s own online application/registration and fee payment after the CUET result process. TISS then published merit lists and conducted document verification at the respective campuses.

The listed 2026 TISS CUET-PG application window ran from May 22, 2026 to June 11, 2026 at 5:00 pm. These dates are historical as of July 19, 2026, and TISS labels its schedule as tentative and subject to change. A future-cycle applicant should wait for the official calendar and follow the route assigned to the chosen programme.
bullets: 1. Choose a TISS programme and verify its entrance route and eligibility.
2. Take the required entrance examination: CUET-PG, CAT, or the specified Non-CUET assessment.
3. Complete the separate TISS programme application when its portal opens, if required for that route.
4. Monitor official merit-list, acceptance, fee-payment, and original-document-verification notices.

sections.heading: Preparation strategy for a former TISSNET aspirant
paragraphs: Replace a TISSNET-centric study plan with preparation for the current route. For a CUET-PG-eligible TISS programme, first locate the prescribed test-paper code and study the NTA syllabus and format for that paper. For the three CAT-PG programmes, prepare for CAT and then follow TISS’s programme-specific shortlist and assessment instructions.

TISS also notes that it receives CUET scores from multiple subject papers and uses a normalisation approach to make results comparable; where a candidate has taken multiple subjects, its 2026 brochure says the best-performing score is considered for the merit list. This makes selecting appropriate, permitted CUET subject papers and carefully checking TISS’s current rules important.
bullets: Start with the official TISS programme list, eligibility criteria, and required test-paper code—not a generic TISSNET syllabus.
Use the official NTA CUET-PG information for CUET preparation and the official CAT materials for CAT-route programmes.
Re-check TISS notices before every application or admission milestone because procedures and dates are cycle-specific.

sections.heading: What successful applicants receive
paragraphs: The entrance route is an admissions mechanism, not a qualification by itself. Successful applicants are offered places in TISS postgraduate programmes subject to merit-list position, the applicable reservation and priority rules, payment and acceptance steps, and document verification.

For CUET-route programmes in 2026, TISS states that reservation and priority rules are applied for final seat allocation through the merit list or waitlist. The institute’s PG programme page lists postgraduate offerings across its campuses; available programmes and their credit structures are subject to the applicable admission cycle and academic framework.
bullets: A qualifying CUET-PG or CAT score does not itself guarantee a TISS offer.
Complete acceptance, fees, and original-document verification within the dates stated in the relevant merit-list notice.
Review the specific TISS programme page for curriculum and campus details rather than inferring them from the former TISSNET exam label.
